stem: A clinical research study measures treatment response rate across 5 trial sites, each containing multiple patient cohorts. trial site A has 10 observations with mean 56.6 % (within-trial site SD = 9.3). trial site B has 8 observations with mean 96.2 % (within-trial site SD = 5.7). trial site C has 15 observations with mean 49.9 % (within-trial site SD = 5.3). trial site D has 5 observations with mean 89.4 % (within-trial site SD = 8.8). trial site E has 8 observations with mean 85.7 % (within-trial site SD = 7.2). The between-trial site standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 6.1 and 15.3 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true treatment response rate for trial site C, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-trial site variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for trial site C: 50.3 % (shrunk from raw 49.9). Range [50.1, 50.9] as between-trial site SD varies from 6.1 to 15.3. Pooled mean: 69.9.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 69.9 % (weighted average of 5 trial site means).
Shrinkage factor for trial site C: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/15). At σ_between=10.2: B = 0.982.
Interval [50.1, 50.9] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (49.9) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (69.9) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
